sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Artist, T1.Famous_Title FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique artists and their famous titles for any musical release that has spent more than one week at the top.

sql: SELECT T1.Song FROM volume AS T1 JOIN music_festival AS T2 ON T1.Volume_ID = T2.Volume WHERE T2.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the songs that were nominated at a music festival.

sql: SELECT Category, COUNT(*) FROM music_festival GROUP BY Category;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each music festival category and the total number of entries within that category.

sql: SELECT T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the songs from artists who are over the age of 30.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top = 3.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the artist and song title for songs that spent exactly 3 weeks at the top of the charts.

sql: SELECT T2.Category, T2.Result FROM volume AS T1 JOIN music_festival AS T2 ON T1.Volume_ID = T2.Volume WHERE T1.Weeks_on_Top = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the award category and outcome for music festivals associated with albums that spent exactly one week at the top of the charts.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artists who have released more than one volume.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artists who have won an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T3.Category FROM volume AS T2 JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume GROUP BY T3.Category ORDER BY avg(T2.Weeks_on_Top) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the music festival category whose songs have the highest average number of weeks spent on top.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Nominated' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an artist who was nominated for a music festival award but did not win.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Famous_Release_date = (SELECT max(Famous_Release_date) FROM artist);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the artist and the title of their work associated with the most recent famous release date in the dataset.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Date_of_ceremony = '18 February 2011' AND T1.Age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique songs performed by artists over the age of 30 at the music festival held on '18 February 2011'.

sql: SELECT Age, count(*) FROM artist WHERE Artist_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Artist_ID FROM volume) GROUP BY Age;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows providing a count of artists for each age, considering only those artists who have created at least one volume.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, sum(T2.Weeks_on_Top) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM music_festival AS T3 WHERE T3.Volume = T2.Volume_ID AND T3.Result = 'Nominated') GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing artists whose volumes have been nominated at a music festival, along with the total cumulative weeks their volumes spent at the top.

sql: SELECT T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T1.Artist = 'Gorgoroth' AND T3.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the song by the artist 'Gorgoroth' that has won an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT Artist FROM artist WHERE Famous_Title LIKE '%Process%' UNION SELECT Artist FROM artist WHERE Age > 34;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artists who either have 'Process' in their famous title or are older than 34.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded' GROUP BY T1.Artist ORDER BY count(T2.Song) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single artist who has the most songs on volumes that received an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T1.Age, T2.Age FROM artist AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist = T2.Artist WHERE T1.Artist_ID < T2.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying artists that have multiple distinct records, showing the artist's name along with the ages listed in each of the two records.

sql: SELECT avg(Age) FROM artist WHERE Artist_ID IN (SELECT Artist_ID FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top >= 3.0);

sql: SELECT Name, Price, Memory_in_G FROM phone WHERE Carrier = 'Sprint';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Num_of_stock FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'IPhone 5s';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price, T2.District FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T1.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T2 ON T3.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID;

sql: SELECT Carrier, AVG(Price) FROM phone GROUP BY Carrier;

sql: SELECT Name FROM phone WHERE Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM phone);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the phone with the highest price.

sql: SELECT T1.District, T1.Num_of_shops FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID WHERE T2.Num_of_stock > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the district and number of shops for markets where the phone stock exceeds 1000 units.

sql: SELECT T1.District, count(T2.Phone_ID) FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T2.Market_ID = (SELECT Market_ID FROM market WHERE Ranking = 1);

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT count(*) FROM phone_market AS T2 WHERE T2.Phone_ID = T1.Phone_ID) FROM phone AS T1;

sql: SELECT T1.District, T3.TotalStock FROM market AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Market_ID, SUM(Num_of_stock) AS TotalStock FROM phone_market GROUP BY Market_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID ORDER BY T3.TotalStock DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price, T2.Num_of_stock FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T1.Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM phone);

result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the districts that do not sell any phones with 32GB of memory.

sql: SELECT District FROM market WHERE Market_ID IN (SELECT T1.Market_ID FROM phone_market AS T1 JOIN phone AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID GROUP BY T1.Market_ID HAVING avg(T2.Price) > 400);

sql: WITH DistrictStoreCategories AS (SELECT T1.District_ID, T1.District_name, T3.Number_of_product_category FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID) SELECT District_name, SUM(Number_of_product_category) FROM DistrictStoreCategories GROUP BY District_name;

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, SUM(T3.Area_size) FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name HAVING SUM(T3.Area_size) > 100;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each district where the total area size of all its stores is greater than 100, along with that summed area size.

sql: SELECT product, pages_per_minute_color FROM product WHERE pages_per_minute_color > (SELECT pages_per_minute_color FROM product WHERE product = 'Xerox Travel Scanner 100');

sql: SELECT District_name AS Name, 'District' AS Type FROM district UNION ALL SELECT Store_Name AS Name, 'Store' AS Type FROM store;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.product FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T1.Type = 'City Mall' AND T1.Ranking > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T4.Product_ID) AS NumberOfProducts FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store_product AS T4 ON T2.Store_ID = T4.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name ORDER BY NumberOfProducts DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each district and the total count of unique products available within it, ordered from the district with the most products to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.artist_name FROM song AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.genre_is = T2.g_name WHERE T1.country = 'Bangladesh' AND T2.rating > '7';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the song name and artist name for any song from Bangladesh that belongs to a genre with a rating greater than 7.

sql: SELECT T1.country, AVG(T2.resolution) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name GROUP BY T1.country HAVING COUNT(T2.song_name) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country that has associated artists with songs, along with the average song resolution for that country.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.country FROM artist AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.artist_name = T1.artist_name AND T2.rating > 7);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and country of artists who have at least one song with a rating greater than 7.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.artist_name FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN files AS T3 ON T1.f_id = T3.f_id WHERE T2.preferred_genre = 'folk' AND T3.formats = 'mp4';

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, COUNT(T2.student_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_name;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.candidate_details, T3.asessment_outcome_code FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T3 ON T2.candidate_id = T3.candidate_id WHERE T3.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass';

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.city FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.city = 'South Minnie';

sql: SELECT T1.course_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T1 GROUP BY T1.course_id HAVING COUNT(T1.student_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T3.course_name = 'statistics';

sql: SELECT T1.person_id, T1.first_name, COUNT(T2.address_id) AS number_of_addresses FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id GROUP BY T1.person_id, T1.first_name;

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM People WHERE person_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Students);

sql: SELECT T1.candidate_id, T1.candidate_details FROM Candidates AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Candidate_Assessments AS T2 WHERE T2.candidate_id = T1.candidate_id AND T2.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the ID and details for candidates who have passed at least one assessment.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN (SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations GROUP BY student_id HAVING count(course_id) > 1) AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id;

sql: SELECT T2.email_address FROM Students AS T1 JOIN People AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.person_id UNION SELECT T2.email_address FROM Candidates AS T1 JOIN People AS T2 ON T1.candidate_id = T2.person_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing a combined, unique list of email addresses for all individuals who are either students or candidates.

sql: SELECT student_id FROM Students INTERSECT SELECT candidate_id FROM Candidates;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the IDs of individuals who are present in both the student and candidate tables.

sql: SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations WHERE course_id = '301' EXCEPT SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations WHERE course_id = '303';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IDs of students who are registered for course '301' but are not registered for course '303'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T4.city FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN People_Addresses AS T3 ON T1.person_id = T3.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.address_id GROUP BY T1.person_id HAVING COUNT(T2.course_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT course_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations GROUP BY course_id HAVING COUNT(student_id) > (SELECT AVG(reg_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(student_id) AS reg_count FROM Student_Course_Registrations GROUP BY course_id) AS T1);

sql: SELECT T1.candidate_id, T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T3.asessment_outcome_code, CASE WHEN T3.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass' THEN 'Certified' ELSE 'Not Certified' END AS certification_status FROM Candidates AS T1 JOIN People AS T2 ON T1.candidate_id = T2.person_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T3 ON T1.candidate_id = T3.candidate_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each candidate's ID, name, and assessment outcome, along with a derived certification status of 'Certified' or 'Not Certified' based on that outcome.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN People AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.person_id WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Student_Course_Attendance AS T3 WHERE T3.student_id = T1.student_id);

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T1.email_address FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN People_Addresses AS T4 ON T1.person_id = T4.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T5 ON T4.address_id = T5.address_id WHERE T5.country = 'USA' AND T3.course_name = 'statistics';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and email address of students from the USA who are registered for the 'statistics' course.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.student_id) AS registered_students, COUNT(T3.date_of_attendance) AS attendance_records FROM Courses AS T1 LEFT JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id LEFT JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T3 ON T1.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, providing a summary for each course that includes its name, the number of unique students registered, and the total count of attendance records.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id WHERE T2.asessment_outcome_code = 'Fail';

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.GDP) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2008;

sql: SELECT T1.City, (T2.Jan + T2.Feb + T2.Mar + T2.Apr + T2.Jun + T2.Jul + T2.Aug + T2.Sep + T2.Oct + T2.Nov + T2.Dec) / 11.0 FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.City, T2.Year FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Venue = 'Estadio Cuscatlán , San Salvador , El Salvador';

sql: SELECT T1.City, T3.Date FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T2.Year = 2009;

sql: SELECT Year, COUNT(Match_ID) FROM hosting_city GROUP BY Year;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T2.Jan > 26;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY T1.City_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Match_ID) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Match_ID IN (SELECT Match_ID FROM match WHERE Result = '2-0');

sql: SELECT T3.Competition, AVG(T1.Regional_Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID GROUP BY T3.Competition;

sql: SELECT City, GDP FROM city WHERE GDP > (SELECT AVG(GDP) FROM city);

sql: SELECT City, Regional_Population, GDP FROM city WHERE Regional_Population > 8500000 UNION SELECT City, Regional_Population, GDP FROM city WHERE GDP > 1000;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM hosting_city AS T2 WHERE T2.Host_City = T1.City_ID);

sql: SELECT T1.City, (T2.Jun + T2.Jul + T2.Aug) / 3.0 AS Avg_Summer_Temp FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID ORDER BY Avg_Summer_Temp DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2008 EXCEPT SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2010;

sql: SELECT T1.Competition FROM match AS T1 WHERE T1.Match_ID IN (SELECT T2.Match_ID FROM hosting_city AS T2 WHERE T2.Host_City = (SELECT City_ID FROM city ORDER BY Regional_Population DESC LIMIT 1));

sql: SELECT T1.City, T3.Date, T3.Score FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification' ORDER BY T3.Date;

sql: SELECT T2.Year, AVG(T1.GDP) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY T2.Year HAVING AVG(T1.GDP) > 700;

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2009 INTERSECT SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2010;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.City, T1.GDP FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T1.GDP < (SELECT MAX(GDP) FROM city);

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.ID) FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID GROUP BY T1.name;

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID JOIN stadium AS T4 ON T3.Stadium_ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.Country = 'Spain';

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID WHERE T2.Event_ID = 1 INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID WHERE T2.Event_ID = 2;

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode WHERE T2.City = 'Aberdeen ';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.AirportName, T2.City FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode;

sql: SELECT T2.Airline, T3.AirportName AS Source, T4.AirportName AS Destination FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T3.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T4 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T4.AirportCode WHERE T1.FlightNo = 28;

sql: SELECT MAX(Cnt) FROM (SELECT count(*) AS Cnt FROM flights GROUP BY Airline) AS T1;

sql: SELECT T3.City, COUNT(*) FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T3.AirportCode WHERE T2.Airline = 'United Airlines' GROUP BY T3.City;

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo FROM flights AS T1 WHERE T1.Airline IN (SELECT T2.Airline FROM flights AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Airline HAVING COUNT(*) > 2);

sql: SELECT T3.Airline, T1.FlightNo, T2.FlightNo, T1.SourceAirport, T1.DestAirport FROM flights AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = TRIM(T2.DestAirport) AND TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = TRIM(T2.SourceAirport) JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid WHERE T1.Airline = T2.Airline AND T1.FlightNo < T2.FlightNo;

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details, T3.Event_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T1.Participant_Type_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the details for participants identified as 'Organizer' and the corresponding details of the events they are associated with.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details, T1.Participant_Type_Code, T3.Event_Details, T4.Service_Type_Code FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.Service_ID = T4.Service_ID WHERE T3.Event_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details and role for each person involved in event number 3, along with that event's details and its associated service type.

sql: SELECT T1.Service_Type_Code, COUNT(T3.Participant_ID) FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Service_Type_Code HAVING COUNT(T3.Participant_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each service type that has had more than one participant across all associated events, along with the total participant count for each type.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Participants_in_Events AS T2 JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T2.Participant_ID = T1.Participant_ID AND T3.Event_Details = 'Success');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the details for all participants who have participated in an event marked as 'Success'.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, COUNT(T2.Participant_ID) FROM (SELECT Event_ID FROM Events WHERE Event_Details = 'Success') AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each successful event by its ID along with the total count of participants for that event.

sql: SELECT Participant_Details FROM Participants WHERE Participant_Type_Code = 'Organizer' UNION SELECT Service_Type_Code FROM Services WHERE Service_Type_Code = 'Marriage';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing a consolidated list of participant details for all 'Organizers' and the 'Marriage' service type code.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_ID FROM Participants AS T1 WHERE T1.Participant_Type_Code = 'Organizer' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Participant_ID FROM Participants_in_Events AS T2 WHERE T2.Event_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of the participant who is both an 'Organizer' and is involved in Event ID 3.

sql: SELECT Participant_Details FROM Participants WHERE Participant_Type_Code = 'Organizer' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID WHERE T2.Event_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the details of all organizers who are not participating in Event 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Event_ID, COUNT(Participant_ID) AS P_Count FROM Participants_in_Events GROUP BY Event_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID WHERE T2.P_Count > (SELECT AVG(P_Count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Participant_ID) AS P_Count FROM Participants_in_Events GROUP BY Event_ID));
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the event ID and details for events with a higher-than-average number of participants.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details, T4.Service_Type_Code, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T4.Service_Type_Code ORDER BY T1.Participant_ID DESC) FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.Service_ID = T4.Service_ID;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows showing participant details and the service type they used, assigning a rank to each participant within each service category based on their ID in descending order.

sql: SELECT Service_Type_Code FROM Services WHERE Service_ID NOT IN (SELECT Service_ID FROM Events WHERE Event_Details = 'Fail');

result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details of participants who have been involved in successful events but have never been involved in any failed events.

sql: SELECT T1.Service_Type_Code FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID WHERE T2.Event_Details = 'Success' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Service_Type_Code FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID WHERE T2.Event_Details = 'Fail';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the service type codes for services that have experienced both a successful event and a failed event.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Country FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Tanzania';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, height, and country for all mountains located in Tanzania.

sql: SELECT Name, Points FROM climber ORDER BY Points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each climber's name and their total points, ordered from the highest number of points to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Country FROM mountain;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique countries that contain mountains.

sql: SELECT Name, Country, Time FROM climber WHERE Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name, country, and recorded time for all climbers from West Germany.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM mountain ORDER BY Height ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and height of the shortest mountain in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows associating each climber's name with the name of the mountain they have climbed.

sql: SELECT T2.Country, COUNT(T1.Climber_ID) FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T2.Country;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country alongside the total count of climbers associated with mountains in that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Points FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Range = 'Kilimanjaro';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and points of climbers who have climbed in the 'Kilimanjaro' mountain range.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Points) FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mountain by name, along with the average points scored by climbers on that specific mountain.

sql: SELECT Name, Points FROM climber WHERE Mountain_ID IN (SELECT Mountain_ID FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Kenya');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and points for each climber who has ascended a mountain located in Kenya.

sql: SELECT T2.Range, SUM(T1.Points) FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T2.Range ORDER BY SUM(T1.Points) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each mountain range and the total points accumulated by climbers on it, ordered from the range with the most points to the least.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Height > (SELECT AVG(Prominence) FROM mountain);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and height of mountains whose height is greater than the average prominence of all mountains in the table.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T2.Mountain_ID, T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Climber_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of mountains that have been climbed by more than one person.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM mountain AS T2 WHERE T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID AND T2.Country = 'Tanzania');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of climbers who have climbed a mountain located in Tanzania.

sql: SELECT Name FROM climber WHERE Mountain_ID = (SELECT Mountain_ID FROM mountain ORDER BY Height DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all climbers who have climbed the single tallest mountain in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Tanzania' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Kenya';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of climbers who have scaled mountains located in either Tanzania or Kenya.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM mountain AS T2 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM climber AS T1 WHERE T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of mountains that have no recorded climbs.

sql: WITH CountryClimbers AS (SELECT T2.Country AS MountainCountry, T1.Name AS ClimberName FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID) SELECT MountainCountry, COUNT(ClimberName) FROM CountryClimbers GROUP BY MountainCountry;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country where mountains were climbed and the total number of climbers for that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Points FROM climber AS T1 WHERE T1.Points > (SELECT AVG(T2.Points) FROM climber AS T2 WHERE T2.Mountain_ID = T1.Mountain_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and points of climbers whose scores were above the average for their respective mountain.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country, T2.ClimberCount FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Mountain_ID, COUNT(*) AS ClimberCount FROM climber GROUP BY Mountain_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID ORDER BY T2.ClimberCount DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mountain's name, its country, and the total number of climbers who have ascended it, ordered from most climbed to least.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Range = 'Kilimanjaro' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM climber WHERE Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of climbers from 'West Germany' who have also climbed a mountain in the 'Kilimanjaro' range.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM mountain AS T1 WHERE T1.Height > (SELECT AVG(T2.Height) FROM mountain AS T2) AND T1.Prominence > (SELECT AVG(T3.Prominence) FROM mountain AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of mountains that have both a height and a prominence greater than the average for all mountains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T1.Climber_ID, T1.Name, T1.Country HAVING SUM(T2.Height) > 5500;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and country of climbers who have ascended a cumulative mountain height greater than 5500.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Country FROM mountain AS T2 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM climber AS T1 WHERE T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID AND T1.Country = T2.Country);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique countries that contain mountains which have not been climbed by any climber from that same country.

sql: SELECT Name, Points FROM climber WHERE Points > (SELECT AVG(Points) FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and points for all climbers who have an above-average point score.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Project) >= (SELECT AVG(proj_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Project) AS proj_count FROM AssignedTo GROUP BY Scientist));

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AssignedTo AS T2 JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T2.Scientist = T1.SSN AND T3.Hours > 200);

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Project WHERE T2.Scientist = 123234877 INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM Projects WHERE Hours < 160;

sql: SELECT Name, Hours FROM Projects WHERE Hours < (SELECT MAX(Hours) FROM Projects) ORDER BY Hours DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Scientist, COUNT(Project) AS PCount FROM AssignedTo GROUP BY Scientist) AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.PCount > 0;

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, T3.course_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T1.student_id = 11;

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, COUNT(T2.course_id) AS NumberOfCourses FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id GROUP BY T1.author_id;

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, T2.date_of_enrolment FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.course_id = 2;

sql: SELECT T2.login_name, T3.course_name, T4.test_result FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T1.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T4 ON T1.registration_id = T4.registration_id WHERE T4.test_result = 'Fail';

sql: SELECT T2.personal_name, T2.family_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id WHERE T1.course_name = 'database';

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id GROUP BY T1.author_id HAVING count(T2.course_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of all individuals who have authored at least one course.

sql: SELECT personal_name, family_name FROM Students WHERE student_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Enrolment WHERE course_id = (SELECT course_id FROM Courses WHERE course_name = 'advanced database'));

sql: SELECT login_name, personal_name, family_name FROM Students WHERE date_of_registration > '2015-10-01' UNION SELECT login_name, personal_name, family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors WHERE gender_mf = '0';

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 WHERE T1.student_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Enrolment) EXCEPT SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of students who are enrolled in at least one course but have not yet taken any tests for their enrollments.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.course_id IN (SELECT course_id FROM Courses WHERE subject_id = (SELECT subject_id FROM Subjects WHERE subject_name = 'Computer Science')) GROUP BY T1.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of all unique students enrolled in at least one 'Computer Science' course.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, T2.EnrolmentCount FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN (SELECT course_id, COUNT(student_id) AS EnrolmentCount FROM Student_Course_Enrolment GROUP BY course_id) AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id ORDER BY T2.EnrolmentCount DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 courses by student enrollment, showing each course's name and total enrollment count ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Courses AS T2 WHERE T2.author_id = T1.author_id AND T2.subject_id = 1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the personal and family names of authors or tutors who have created at least one course for the subject with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.subject_id) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who are enrolled in courses from at least two different subjects.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 WHERE T2.student_id = T1.student_id) AS NumberOfEnrolments FROM Students AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each student's first and last name, along with the total number of courses they are enrolled in.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 WHERE T1.student_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.student_id FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id WHERE T3.test_result = 'Fail');
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the first and last names of all students who have never failed a test.

sql: SELECT T4.subject_name, T3.test_result, COUNT(*) AS test_count FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T1.registration_id = T3.registration_id JOIN Subjects AS T4 ON T2.subject_id = T4.subject_id GROUP BY T4.subject_name, T3.test_result;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each academic subject and its corresponding count of 'Pass' and 'Fail' test results.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 WHERE T2.student_id = T1.student_id);

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, T4.course_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T2.course_id = T4.course_id WHERE T3.test_result = 'Pass' AND T4.subject_id = 1;

sql: SELECT T2.Built_Year, COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Built_Year;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship's build year alongside the total number of captains assigned to ships from that year.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM Ship WHERE Built_Year = 1997.0);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of captains who command ships that were built in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT T1.Class, AVG(CAST(T1.age AS INTEGER)) FROM captain AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Class;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each ship class and the corresponding average age of the captains for that class.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman' UNION SELECT Name FROM Ship WHERE Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows containing a combined list of names, which includes all captains with the rank 'Midshipman' and all ships flying the 'Panama' flag.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.age FROM captain AS T1 WHERE T1.Ship_ID = (SELECT Ship_ID FROM Ship ORDER BY Built_Year ASC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and age of the captain commanding the oldest ship, as determined by the earliest build year.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Class, T2.Flag FROM Ship AS T2 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM captain AS T1 WHERE T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID AND T1.age > 40);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the distinct class and flag for any ship that has a captain over the age of 40.

sql: SELECT T1.Class, COUNT(*) FROM captain AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Class HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying ship classes commanded by more than one captain, along with the total count of captains for each identified class.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.age FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = (SELECT MIN(Built_Year) FROM Ship);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and ages of the captains who command the oldest ship(s), based on the earliest build year.

sql: WITH ShipCaptainCount AS (SELECT T2.Name AS ShipName, COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) as CaptainCount FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Name) SELECT ShipName, CaptainCount FROM ShipCaptainCount WHERE CaptainCount >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each ship with at least one captain, along with the total count of captains assigned to that ship.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.age FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'HMS Gorgon';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and age of every captain, excluding any who are in command of the ship 'HMS Gorgon'.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE age = (SELECT MAX(age) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and age of the oldest captain in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Class = 'Third-rate ship of the line' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the names of captains and their ships, specifically for those who are both of the 'Third-rate ship of the line' class and command a ship flying the 'Panama' flag.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.age FROM captain AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT T2.Ship_ID FROM Ship AS T2 WHERE T2.Ship_ID = T1.Ship_ID AND T2.Type = 'Panamax');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and age for each captain who commands a 'Panamax' type ship.

sql: SELECT T1.Class, T2.Flag, COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Class, T2.Flag;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows providing a count of captains for each combination of captain class and ship flag.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 WHERE CAST(T1.age AS INTEGER) > ( SELECT AVG(CAST(T2.age AS INTEGER)) FROM captain AS T2 WHERE T2.Class = T1.Class );
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of captains whose age is greater than the average age of all captains within their same class.

sql: SELECT Name, Class FROM captain WHERE Class <> 'Cutter' UNION ALL SELECT Name, Type FROM Ship WHERE Built_Year = 1998.0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows that combine two distinct lists: the names and classes of all captains not in the 'Cutter' class, along with the names and types of all ships built in the year 1998.

sql: SELECT Name, age, CASE WHEN CAST(age AS INTEGER) > 50 THEN 'Veteran' WHEN CAST(age AS INTEGER) > 40 THEN 'Experienced' ELSE 'Junior' END AS Experience FROM captain ORDER BY CAST(age AS INTEGER) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each captain's name, age, and a calculated experience level ('Veteran', 'Experienced', or 'Junior') based on their age, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing all available details for students who reside in the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing all known allergies that are categorized as 'food'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID = 1002;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name for the student with an ID of 1002.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Allergy FROM Has_Allergy;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, providing a unique list of all recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > 18;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of all students who are older than 18.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Allergy FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 58 rows, with each row linking a student's first and last name to one of their specific, recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, COUNT(T2.Allergy) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 29 rows listing each student's first and last name along with a total count of their recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T3.Allergy, T3.AllergyType FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T1.city_code = 'HKG';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first name, specific allergy, and allergy type for all students located in the city with the code 'HKG'.

sql: SELECT Major, COUNT(*) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each academic major along with the total number of students enrolled in that major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 28 rows listing the first and last names of all students who have a food-related allergy.

sql: SELECT Major, AVG(Age) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each major along with the average age of students for that major.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy WHERE Allergy = 'Cat');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all students who are allergic to cats.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING COUNT(T2.Allergy) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the first and last names of students who have more than one recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT Major FROM Student GROUP BY Major HAVING AVG(Age) < 20;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the student majors where the average age of students in that major is less than 20.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Cat';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the first and last names of students who have a food allergy but are not allergic to cats.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Allergy) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the first and last name of the student with the highest number of allergies.

sql: SELECT T3.AllergyType, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.StuID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy GROUP BY T3.AllergyType;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, providing a count of the unique students associated with each allergy type.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = (SELECT city_code FROM Student WHERE Fname = 'Linda' AND LName = 'Smith') AND StuID != 1001;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students who live in the same city as 'Linda Smith', excluding the student with ID 1001.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy NOT IN (SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food');
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing the unique first and last names of students who have at least one recorded non-food allergy.

sql: SELECT Major FROM Student GROUP BY Major ORDER BY COUNT(StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single major with the highest number of students.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Advisor FROM Student WHERE Advisor IN (SELECT Advisor FROM Student GROUP BY Advisor HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing the first name, last name, and advisor ID for every student who shares an advisor with at least one other student.

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Allergy) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a count of the total number of unique allergies found among all female students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Allergy FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the first name, last name, and allergy for students whose age is greater than the average age of all students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.AllergyType) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of students who have more than one distinct type of allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID AND T3.AllergyType = 'food');
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the first and last names of all students who have a recorded food allergy.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Chair_Name, Location FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the festival name, chair name, and location for all festivals held in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artworks that are classified as 'Program Music/Variety Show'.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail ORDER BY Num_of_Audience DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each festival's name and its total number of audience members, ordered from the highest attendance to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Result FROM nomination;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the distinct types of results for a nomination (e.g., 'Nominated', 'Won').

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Year FROM festival_detail WHERE Chair_Name = 'Raymond Floyd';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and year of the festival chaired by 'Raymond Floyd'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks that have won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, count(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each festival name along with the total count of artwork nominations it has received.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, count(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Year;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each festival year along with the total count of artwork nominations for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Festival_Name, T2.Result FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each artwork, the festival it was nominated for, and the result of that nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for festivals held in the United States.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail WHERE Num_of_Audience > (SELECT MIN(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and audience count for all festivals, excluding the festival with the smallest audience.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, COUNT(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID GROUP BY T1.Type;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artwork type along with the total number of nominations received for that type.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Artwork_ID IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Nominated');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all artworks that have been nominated for an award.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Artwork_ID NOT IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of artworks that have never won a nomination.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Year FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nomination AS T2 WHERE T2.Festival_ID = T1.Festival_ID AND T2.Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and year of each festival that has had at least one winning nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Chair_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_ID HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the chair names for festivals that have received more than one nomination.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail WHERE Num_of_Audience > (SELECT AVG(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and audience counts for festivals with an attendance greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, (SELECT count(*) FROM nomination AS T2 WHERE T2.Festival_ID = T1.Festival_ID) AS Nomination_Count FROM festival_detail AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each festival's name and the total count of nominations it received.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail ORDER BY Num_of_Audience ASC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks nominated for the festival that had the lowest number of audience members.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Nominated' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for an award but did not win.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Year = 2007 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of an artwork that won an award and was also nominated for a festival in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_Name = 'Flower Awards');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and type of artworks that were nominated for the 'Flower Awards' festival.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show' UNION SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2006;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing a combined list of unique names from two sources: artworks classified as 'Program Music/Variety Show' and festivals that occurred in the year 2006.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won' AND T3.Chair_Name = 'Charles Coody';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the artwork that won a nomination at a festival chaired by 'Charles Coody'.

sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007) AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID;
result_summary: Returns a single row showing the total count of artwork nominations for all festivals that occurred in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT * FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows containing all available information for each person, including their ID, sex, name, date of birth, height, and weight.

sql: SELECT Name, Date_of_Birth FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and date of birth for all individuals identified as male.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, Support_rate FROM candidate WHERE Candidate_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the poll source and the corresponding support rate for the specific candidate with ID 1.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Poll_Source FROM candidate;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique poll source from which candidate data was gathered.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM candidate WHERE Date = 'Feb 12–15, 2007';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the total count of candidates recorded for the date 'Feb 12–15, 2007'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Support_rate, T2.Oppose_rate FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name, support rate, and opposition rate for each person who is a candidate.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each poll source along with the average candidate support rate calculated from that source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the name, height, and weight of candidates who were included in the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT Sex, COUNT(*) FROM people GROUP BY Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each gender and the total number of people corresponding to that gender.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T2.Support_rate DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of candidates, ordered by their support rate from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT Date, SUM(Unsure_rate) FROM candidate GROUP BY Date;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each date along with the total unsure rate for all candidates on that day.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate > 0.17;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of candidates who have a support rate greater than 17%.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate = (SELECT MAX(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the candidate with the highest support rate.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Oppose_rate > (SELECT AVG(Oppose_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of candidates whose opposition rate is greater than the average opposition rate of all candidates.

sql: SELECT T2.Poll_Source, COUNT(T1.People_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Poll_Source HAVING COUNT(T1.People_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing poll sources that have identified two or more candidates, along with the total count of candidates for each of those sources.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people WHERE Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and heights for all people who are taller than the overall average height.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate < 0.2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a candidate from the 'WNBC/Marist Poll' whose support rate is 20% or greater.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.s_rate FROM people AS T1 JOIN (SELECT People_ID, Support_rate AS s_rate FROM candidate WHERE Support_rate > 0.17) AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and support rate for each candidate whose support rate is greater than 17%.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Sex = 'M' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the male candidate who was also included in the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Support_rate FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T2.Support_rate DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and support rate of the candidate with the second-highest support rate.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate > 0.2 UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Height > 190;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows containing a unique list of names for people who are either candidates with a support rate over 20% or are individuals taller than 190.

sql: WITH AvgRates AS (SELECT Poll_Source, AVG(Support_rate) AS avg_sup FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source) SELECT T2.Name, T3.Date, T3.Support_rate FROM AvgRates AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T3 ON T1.Poll_Source = T3.Poll_Source JOIN people AS T2 ON T3.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T3.Support_rate > T1.avg_sup;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the candidate's name, poll date, and support rate for polls where their performance exceeded the average support rate for that specific polling source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID NOT IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate WHERE Oppose_rate <= 0.4);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name, height, and weight of individuals, excluding any candidates who have an opposition rate of 40% or less.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM candidate AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID AND T2.Unsure_rate > 0.5);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any person who is also a candidate with an unsure rate greater than 50%.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Sex FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and sex of candidates who were polled by the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT customer_id, first_name, last_name, date_became_customer FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing 'good' customers, including their ID, first and last name, and the date they first became a customer.

sql: SELECT product_name, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_type_code = 'Cutlery';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the product name and daily hire cost for all products categorized as 'Cutlery'.

sql: SELECT booking_id, customer_id, amount_payable FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows for all confirmed bookings, listing the booking ID, customer ID, and the amount payable for each booking.

sql: SELECT payment_id, booking_id, amount_paid FROM Payments WHERE payment_type_code = 'Cash';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the payment ID, booking ID, and the amount paid for all transactions made by cash.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_type_code, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire ORDER BY daily_hire_cost DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing products available for hire with their name, type code, and daily hire cost, ordered from the most to least expensive.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.booking_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each customer by first and last name, along with a total count of the bookings they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id, SUM(T2.amount_paid) FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id GROUP BY T1.booking_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each booking ID along with the total amount paid for that booking.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T4.product_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each customer's first and last name paired with the name of a product they have booked.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_code, COUNT(T2.product_id) FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each product type code along with the total number of bookings for all products within that type.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.coupon_amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Discount_Coupons AS T2 ON T1.coupon_id = T2.coupon_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer by first and last name, along with the monetary value of the specific discount coupon assigned to them.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.amount_paid) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.payment_type_code = 'Credit Card' GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each customer's first and last name along with the total amount they have paid using a credit card.

sql: SELECT AVG(amount_of_discount) FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Returns a single value representing the average discount amount for all confirmed bookings.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Products_Booked WHERE returned_late_yn = '1') GROUP BY T1.product_id HAVING COUNT(T2.booking_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING SUM(T2.amount_paid) > (SELECT AVG(amount_paid) FROM Payments);
