sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.train_number FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and number of each train that serves the station with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, T2.high_temperature FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.day_of_week = 'Tuesday' AND T2.high_temperature > 60;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the station network name and its high temperature for any Tuesday where the temperature was over 60 degrees.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.station_id) AS station_count FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each train by name along with the total number of stations on its route.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, AVG(T2.high_temperature) AS avg_high_temp FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each station network name along with its corresponding average weekly high temperature.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name, T1.origin FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T3.local_authority = 'Watford';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique name and origin combinations for trains that pass through a station in the 'Watford' local authority.

sql: SELECT name, train_number FROM train WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT train_id FROM route);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and train number for any train that has not been assigned a route.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name HAVING AVG(T2.wind_speed_mph) > 12;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of station networks where the average weekly wind speed exceeds 12 mph.

sql: SELECT origin FROM train UNION SELECT destination FROM train;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows providing a combined, unique list of all cities that serve as either an origin or a destination for a train.

sql: SELECT name FROM train WHERE interval = 'Daily' EXCEPT SELECT name FROM train WHERE origin = 'Trivandrum';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of trains that run daily but do not originate from Trivandrum.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.station_id FROM route AS T2 WHERE T2.train_id = (SELECT T3.id FROM train AS T3 WHERE T3.name = 'Ananthapuri Express'));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the network names for all stations served by the 'Ananthapuri Express' train.

sql: WITH TempRange AS (SELECT station_id, high_temperature - low_temperature AS temp_diff FROM weekly_weather) SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN TempRange AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name ORDER BY AVG(T2.temp_diff) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the station network that has the greatest average weekly temperature difference (high minus low), ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM route AS T2 JOIN weekly_weather AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.station_id WHERE T2.train_id = T1.id AND T3.precipitation >= 90.0);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of trains whose routes include at least one station experiencing heavy weekly precipitation (90.0 or higher).

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, T1.services FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.precipitation = (SELECT MAX(precipitation) FROM weekly_weather);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the network name and services for the station(s) that experienced the highest recorded weekly precipitation.

sql: SELECT T4.day_of_week, T4.precipitation, T4.wind_speed_mph FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id JOIN weekly_weather AS T4 ON T3.id = T4.station_id WHERE T1.name = 'Ananthapuri Express' AND T3.local_authority = 'Chiltern';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows showing the day of the week, precipitation, and wind speed for stations located in the 'Chiltern' local authority that are served by the 'Ananthapuri Express' train.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.wind_speed_mph > 13 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS WindyDays FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name HAVING WindyDays > 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each station network that had at least one windy week, along with a total count of weeks where wind speed exceeded 13 mph for that network.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, T2.max_high FROM station AS T1 JOIN (SELECT station_id, MAX(high_temperature) AS max_high FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY station_id) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.max_high > 65;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the network name and all-time maximum high temperature for each weather station that has ever recorded a high temperature above 65 degrees.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.origin FROM train AS T1 WHERE (SELECT count(*) FROM route AS T2 WHERE T2.train_id = T1.id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and origin of trains that are associated with more than one route.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 WHERE T1.services = 'Metropolitan line and Chiltern Railways' INTERSECT SELECT T2.network_name FROM station AS T2 JOIN weekly_weather AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.station_id WHERE T3.day_of_week = 'Monday' AND T3.high_temperature = 59;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the network name of any station that both provides 'Metropolitan line and Chiltern Railways' services and also had a high temperature of 59 on a Monday.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY station_id HAVING AVG(precipitation) >= (SELECT AVG(precipitation) FROM weekly_weather));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of trains whose routes include stations with an average precipitation greater than or equal to the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T4.high_temperature) FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id JOIN weekly_weather AS T4 ON T3.id = T4.station_id WHERE T4.day_of_week = 'Monday' GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each train's name and the average high temperature on Mondays across all stations on its route.

sql: SELECT customer_details FROM Customers WHERE customer_id = 5;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the customer details for the specific customer with an ID of 5.

sql: SELECT property_address, other_details FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code = 'house';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the address and other details for all properties classified as a 'house'.

sql: SELECT service_id, service_details FROM Services WHERE service_type_code = 'Check';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the service ID and details for all services of the type 'Check'.

sql: SELECT thing_id, service_type_code FROM Things WHERE Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Electronics';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID and associated service type code for all items that are classified as 'Electronics'.

sql: SELECT Status_of_Thing_Code, Date_and_Date FROM Timed_Status_of_Things WHERE thing_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the status history for a specific thing, providing each status code and the date it was recorded.

sql: SELECT T2.property_address, COUNT(T1.resident_id) FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Properties AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id GROUP BY T2.property_address;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows, listing each property address along with the total number of residents for that property.

sql: SELECT T2.organization_details, COUNT(T1.service_id) FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id GROUP BY T2.organization_details;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each organization's details along with a total count of the services offered by that organization.

sql: SELECT T3.organization_details, T2.service_type_code FROM Residents_Services AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id JOIN Organizations AS T3 ON T2.organization_id = T3.organization_id WHERE T1.other_details = 'Unsatisfied';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the organizations and their service types for all resident interactions that were marked as 'Unsatisfied'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.resident_id = 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details of the customer involved in events for resident ID 10.

sql: SELECT T1.Type_of_Thing_Code, T1.service_details FROM Things AS T1 JOIN Timed_Status_of_Things AS T2 ON T1.thing_id = T2.thing_id WHERE T2.Status_of_Thing_Code = 'Open';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the type code and service details for all 'things' that currently have an 'Open' status.

sql: SELECT T2.organization_details, COUNT(T1.thing_id) FROM Things AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id GROUP BY T2.organization_details HAVING COUNT(T1.thing_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing organizations that have more than one associated 'thing', along with the total count of things for each of those organizations.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details AS Child_Organization, T2.organization_details AS Parent_Organization FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.parent_organization_id = T2.organization_id WHERE T1.organization_id != T2.organization_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each child organization alongside its corresponding parent organization to illustrate the corporate hierarchy.

sql: SELECT T1.property_type_code, COUNT(T3.Customer_Event_ID) FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Customer_Events AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id WHERE T1.property_type_code IN ('house', 'apartment') GROUP BY T1.property_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total count of customer events broken down by residential property type, specifically for 'houses' and 'apartments'.

sql: SELECT T5.organization_details FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.service_id = T4.service_id JOIN Organizations AS T5 ON T4.organization_id = T5.organization_id WHERE T1.property_type_code = 'house' INTERSECT SELECT T2.organization_details FROM Services AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id WHERE T1.service_type_code = 'Check';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details of organizations that provide 'Check' services and also serve residents who live in houses.

sql: SELECT customer_details FROM Customers WHERE customer_id = (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Events GROUP BY customer_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details for the most active customer, identified by having the highest number of recorded events.

sql: SELECT T1.property_address FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.date_moved_in > '2017-01-01 00:00:00' EXCEPT SELECT T1.property_address FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.service_id = T4.service_id WHERE T4.service_type_code = 'Cleanning';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the address of a property where a resident moved in after 2017, but which does not have any residents signed up for the 'Cleaning' service.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Things AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id WHERE T2.thing_id IN (SELECT thing_id FROM Timed_Status_of_Things WHERE Status_of_Thing_Code = 'In Progress') OR T2.thing_id IN (SELECT thing_id FROM Timed_Locations_of_Things GROUP BY thing_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique details of organizations that are associated with things either currently 'In Progress' or that have been recorded in multiple locations.

sql: WITH RankedEvents AS (SELECT T1.property_type_code, T3.other_details AS resident_name, COUNT(T4.Customer_Event_ID) AS EventCount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY T1.property_type_code ORDER BY COUNT(T4.Customer_Event_ID) DESC) as rn FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Residents AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id JOIN Customer_Events AS T4 ON T2.resident_id = T4.resident_id GROUP BY T1.property_type_code, T3.other_details) SELECT property_type_code, resident_name, EventCount FROM RankedEvents WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the resident with the most customer events for each property type, showing their name and total event count.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Things AS T3 ON T2.thing_id = T3.thing_id JOIN Organizations AS T4 ON T3.organization_id = T4.organization_id WHERE T4.organization_details = 'Denesik and Sons Party' EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Things AS T3 ON T2.thing_id = T3.thing_id JOIN Organizations AS T4 ON T3.organization_id = T4.organization_id WHERE T4.organization_details = 'Reinger, Hudson and Nolan Group';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the details for customers associated with the 'Denesik and Sons Party' organization, excluding any who are also associated with the 'Reinger, Hudson and Nolan Group' organization.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.service_type_code FROM Customer_Event_Notes AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.Customer_Event_ID = T2.Customer_Event_ID JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T2.resident_id = T3.resident_id JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.service_id = T4.service_id WHERE T2.date_moved_in = '2015-03-27 12:00:00' AND T1.service_type_code = 'Check';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique service type for residents who moved in on March 27, 2015 and have a customer event note of type 'Check'.

sql: SELECT T2.organization_details FROM Things AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id WHERE T1.thing_id = (SELECT thing_id FROM Timed_Status_of_Things GROUP BY thing_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the organizational details for the owner of the 'thing' with the most recorded status updates.

sql: SELECT T2.organization_details, COUNT(T3.service_id) FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.parent_organization_id = T2.organization_id LEFT JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.organization_id = T3.organization_id WHERE T1.organization_id != T1.parent_organization_id GROUP BY T2.organization_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details for each parent organization along with the total count of services associated with that parent.

sql: SELECT T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Properties AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.property_type_code = 'apartment' INTERSECT SELECT T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id WHERE T2.other_details = 'Unsatisfied';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details of residents who both live in an apartment and have a service record marked as 'Unsatisfied'.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Event_ID FROM Customer_Event_Notes AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Customer_Event_ID HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T1.service_type_code = 'Check' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 AND SUM(CASE WHEN T1.service_type_code = 'Cleanning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the customer event IDs for events that included both a 'Check' service and a 'Cleaning' service.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN (SELECT thing_id FROM Timed_Status_of_Things AS T_sub WHERE T_sub.Date_and_Date = (SELECT MAX(Date_and_Date) FROM Timed_Status_of_Things WHERE thing_id = T_sub.thing_id) AND T_sub.Status_of_Thing_Code = 'Close') AS T3 ON T2.thing_id = T3.thing_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique details of customers who are associated with events where the involved item's most recent status is 'Close'.

sql: SELECT Name, Level FROM member WHERE Hometown = 'Louisville, Kentucky';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and level for each member whose hometown is 'Louisville, Kentucky'.

sql: SELECT Name, Open_year FROM branch WHERE City = 'London';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and opening year for all branches located in the city of London.

sql: SELECT Name, Card_Number FROM member ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and card number for each member, ordered alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT Member_ID, Total_pounds FROM purchase WHERE Year = '2017';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the Member ID and the total purchase weight in pounds for a transaction that occurred in 2017.

sql: SELECT Name, membership_amount FROM branch ORDER BY membership_amount DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each branch's name and its membership amount, ordered from the highest to the lowest amount.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Register_Year FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Register_Year = '2016';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all members who registered in the year 2016.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Total_pounds) FROM member AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each member by name along with the total pounds from all of their purchases.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Member_ID) FROM branch AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Branch_ID = T2.Branch_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each branch name along with the total count of members registered to that branch.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_pounds FROM member AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Level = 6;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the individual purchase amounts (in pounds) and the corresponding names for all members at Level 6.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Total_pounds) FROM member AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Hometown = 'Louisville, Kentucky';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average total purchase amount in pounds for all members from Louisville, Kentucky.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM branch AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Branch_ID = T2.Branch_ID WHERE T2.Year = '2017';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the branch that processed a purchase in the year 2017.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Branch_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of all members registered at the branch with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN branch AS T3 ON T2.Branch_ID = T3.Branch_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Alexandre';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all members who are registered at the 'Alexandre' branch.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN branch AS T3 ON T2.Branch_ID = T3.Branch_ID WHERE T1.Hometown = 'Louisville, Kentucky';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of branches that have members from 'Louisville, Kentucky'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM branch AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Branch_ID FROM membership_register_branch GROUP BY Branch_ID HAVING COUNT(Member_ID) > 1) AS T2 ON T1.Branch_ID = T2.Branch_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of each branch that has more than one registered member.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Level FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM purchase WHERE Total_pounds > 1000);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and level of any member who has made a purchase exceeding 1000 pounds.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM purchase AS T2 WHERE T2.Member_ID = T1.Member_ID AND T2.Year = '2018');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of members who made at least one purchase during the year 2018.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 LEFT JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Member_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of members who have never made a purchase.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM membership_register_branch WHERE Register_Year = '2016') EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM purchase WHERE Year = '2017');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of members who registered in 2016 but did not make any purchases in 2017.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Open_year FROM branch AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Branch_ID = T2.Branch_ID WHERE T2.Total_pounds = (SELECT MAX(Total_pounds) FROM purchase);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and opening year of the branch associated with the single largest purchase measured by total pounds.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN branch AS T3 ON T2.Branch_ID = T3.Branch_ID WHERE T1.Level = 6 ORDER BY T2.Total_pounds DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the Level 6 member and the branch name associated with the single largest purchase, determined by the total pounds spent.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN purchase AS T3 ON T1.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T2.Branch_ID <> T3.Branch_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of members who have made a purchase at a branch different from their membership registration branch.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Member_ID FROM purchase GROUP BY Member_ID HAVING SUM(Total_pounds) > 300) AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Register_Year = '2017';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a member who both registered in the year 2017 and has spent a total of more than 300 pounds on purchases.

sql: SELECT Year FROM purchase GROUP BY Year HAVING AVG(Total_pounds) > (SELECT AVG(Total_pounds) FROM purchase);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the year in which the average purchase weight per transaction exceeded the overall average purchase weight across all years.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Open_year FROM branch AS T1 WHERE T1.membership_amount > (SELECT AVG(membership_amount) FROM branch WHERE City = 'London');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and opening year of branches whose membership amount is greater than the average membership amount of all branches in London.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, email_address FROM Customers WHERE customer_status_code = 'Bad Customer';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first name, last name, and email address for all customers who have a status of 'Bad Customer'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT vehicle_details FROM Vehicles;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying each distinct type of vehicle.

sql: SELECT customer_id, amount_payment, datetime_payment FROM Customer_Payments WHERE payment_method_code = 'Direct Debit';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing payments made via 'Direct Debit', showing the corresponding customer ID, payment amount, and the transaction's date and time.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, date_joined_staff FROM Staff WHERE date_joined_staff > '2018-01-01';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first name, last name, and join date for the staff member hired after January 1, 2018.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.city = 'Port Melyssa';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of customers who live in the city of 'Port Melyssa'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.lesson_id) FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each staff member's first and last name along with the total count of lessons they have conducted.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.amount_payment) AS total_paid FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each customer's first and last name, along with the total payment amount calculated for that customer.

sql: SELECT T1.vehicle_details, COUNT(T2.lesson_id) AS number_of_lessons FROM Vehicles AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.vehicle_id = T2.vehicle_id GROUP BY T1.vehicle_details;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total number of lessons conducted for each vehicle type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.lesson_status_code = 'Cancelled';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique first and last names of staff members who have had at least one lesson with a 'Cancelled' status.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, AVG(T1.price) AS average_price FROM Lessons AS T1 GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T1.lesson_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing customers who have taken more than one lesson, showing each customer's ID alongside the average price they paid for their lessons.

sql: SELECT T2.city, SUM(T1.amount_outstanding) AS total_outstanding FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id GROUP BY T2.city;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each city along with the total outstanding financial amount owed by all customers located in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T1.amount_outstanding, SUM(T2.price) AS total_lesson_cost FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's name, their outstanding balance, and the total calculated cost of all lessons they have purchased.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.amount_payment > (SELECT AVG(amount_payment) FROM Customer_Payments);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of customers who have made at least one payment that is greater than the average payment amount.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.staff_address_id = T2.address_id JOIN Lessons AS T3 ON T1.staff_id = T3.staff_id JOIN Vehicles AS T4 ON T3.vehicle_id = T4.vehicle_id WHERE T2.state_province_county = 'Georgia' AND T4.vehicle_details = 'Car';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of a staff member from Georgia who teaches lessons using a car.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, SUM(price) AS total_spent FROM Lessons GROUP BY customer_id) AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.customer_status_code = 'Good Customer' AND T2.total_spent > (SELECT AVG(price) FROM Lessons);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of customers in 'Good Customer' standing whose total spending on lessons exceeds the average price of a single lesson.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.lesson_id) AS lesson_count FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id ORDER BY lesson_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first name, last name, and total lesson count for the staff member who has taught the most lessons.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T1.amount_outstanding, SUM(T2.amount_payment) AS total_paid FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T1.amount_outstanding HAVING SUM(T2.amount_payment) < T1.amount_outstanding;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying customers whose total payments are less than their outstanding balance, showing the customer's name, the outstanding amount, and the total they have paid.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.country = 'USA' AND T1.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM Lessons);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of all customers located in the USA who have not taken any lessons.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Staff WHERE staff_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT staff_id FROM Lessons) UNION SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Customers WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM Lessons);
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the first and last names of all unique staff members and customers who have participated in at least one lesson.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_status_code = 'Bad Customer' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Customer_Payments AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of customers who are designated as 'Bad Customer' but have a record of making at least one payment.

sql: SELECT customer_id, datetime_payment, amount_payment, SUM(amount_payment) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY datetime_payment) as running_total FROM Customer_Payments;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows detailing every customer payment, including the payment timestamp and amount, along with a running total of payments calculated chronologically for each customer.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.city IN (SELECT T4.city FROM Staff AS T3 JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.staff_address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T3.first_name = 'Kaitlin');

sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', lesson_date) AS month, SUM(price) AS total_price FROM Lessons GROUP BY month ORDER BY total_price DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single month with the highest total revenue from lessons, showing the month and the corresponding sum of all lesson prices.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 WHERE T1.staff_id IN (SELECT T2.staff_id FROM Lessons AS T2 JOIN Vehicles AS T3 ON T2.vehicle_id = T3.vehicle_id WHERE T3.vehicle_details = 'Truck') EXCEPT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 WHERE T1.staff_id IN (SELECT T2.staff_id FROM Lessons AS T2 JOIN Vehicles AS T3 ON T2.vehicle_id = T3.vehicle_id WHERE T3.vehicle_details != 'Truck');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have exclusively taught lessons using trucks.

sql: SELECT T2.vehicle_details, COUNT(T1.lesson_id) FROM Lessons AS T1 JOIN Vehicles AS T2 ON T1.vehicle_id = T2.vehicle_id WHERE T1.staff_id IN (SELECT staff_id FROM Staff WHERE date_joined_staff > '2017-01-01 00:00:00') GROUP BY T2.vehicle_details;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each vehicle type and the total number of lessons taught using that vehicle by staff hired after January 1st, 2017.
